metadata.title: BRD4 Inhibitors in Hematologic Malignancies: A Comparative Study

chunks.content: BET bromodomain inhibitors represent an emerging class of epigenetic therapies for hematologic malignancies. In this multicenter comparative study, we evaluated the efficacy and safety of three BRD4 inhibitors—NVX-0228, OTX-015, and PLX-2853—in patients with relapsed or refractory hematologic malignancies. NVX-0228 demonstrated an IC50 of 15.3 nM against BRD4-BD1 in our standardized head-to-head biochemical comparison, molecular weight 489.1 g/mol, and was the most potent agent in ex vivo primary patient sample assays. The Phase II trial (NCT05234567) of NVX-0228 in r/r DLBCL showed an overall response rate (ORR) of 38%.

Despite the promise of BET inhibitors in hematologic malignancies, no agent has yet received regulatory approval in this setting. Clinical development has been challenged by overlapping toxicity profiles, particularly thrombocytopenia and gastrointestinal adverse events, and by the emergence of acquired resistance. This study was designed to provide a standardized comparison of three clinical-stage BRD4 inhibitors across a common panel of biochemical, cellular, and clinical endpoints. All biochemical assays were performed at a single central laboratory using validated protocols to enable direct comparison.

Biochemical characterization was performed using a unified AlphaScreen assay platform with recombinant full-length BRD4 protein expressed in Sf9 cells. NVX-0228 yielded an IC50 of 15.3 nM (95% CI: 12.1-19.3 nM) against BRD4-BD1 in this standardized assay. We note that the molecular weight of NVX-0228 used in our study was 489.1 g/mol based on mass spectrometry analysis of the clinical supply material, which includes the dihydrochloride salt form used in clinical dosing. This may differ from the free base molecular weight reported by the originating group.

In the Phase II multicenter trial (NCT05234567), 250 patients with relapsed or refractory hematologic malignancies were enrolled across 28 sites. The NVX-0228 cohort (n=82) received 200 mg BID and showed an ORR of 38% in DLBCL (n=52), including 8% complete responses. Activity was particularly notable in MYC-amplified tumors, with an ORR of 56% in the MYC-amplified subset (n=18) compared to 29% in non-MYC-amplified cases (n=34). The NVX-0228 cohort in AML (n=30) showed an ORR of 40%, consistent with prior Phase I data.

Table 3. Comparative Safety Profile of BRD4 Inhibitors (All Grades / Grade 3-4)

Adverse Event | NVX-0228 (n=82) | OTX-015 (n=84) | PLX-2853 (n=84)
Thrombocytopenia | 41% / 8% | 58% / 22% | 35% / 6%
Neutropenia | 22% / 5% | 31% / 12% | 18% / 3%
Anemia | 28% / 4% | 25% / 6% | 30% / 5%
Diarrhea | 24% / 2% | 18% / 1% | 32% / 4%
Fatigue | 30% / 1% | 27% / 2% | 25% / 1%
Nausea | 18% / 0% | 22% / 1% | 15% / 0%
Elevated ALT | 12% / 2% | 8% / 1% | 15% / 3%

Severe thrombocytopenia (Grade 3-4) occurred in 8% of NVX-0228-treated subjects, which was significantly lower than OTX-015 (22%, p=0.012) but not significantly different from PLX-2853 (6%, p=0.58).

Analysis of paired baseline and progression biopsies from 14 patients who initially responded to NVX-0228 but subsequently progressed revealed acquired resistance mechanisms. The most common mechanism was BRD4 phosphorylation at serine residues S492 and S498, detected in 9/14 (64%) of progression samples but 0/14 matched baseline samples. Phosphorylation at these sites was shown to alter the conformation of the BD1 bromodomain, reducing NVX-0228 binding affinity by approximately 40-fold. Additional resistance mechanisms included SPOP mutations (3/14, 21%) and MYC amplification independent of BRD4 (2/14, 14%).

Table 5. Response by Molecular Subtype (NVX-0228 Cohort)

Subtype | N | ORR | CR | Median PFS (months)
MYC-amplified DLBCL | 18 | 56% | 11% | 5.8
Non-MYC DLBCL | 34 | 29% | 6% | 3.1
AML (all) | 30 | 40% | 7% | 4.5
AML with FLT3-ITD | 8 | 50% | 12% | 5.2
AML without FLT3-ITD | 22 | 36% | 5% | 4.0

PFS = progression-free survival; CR = complete response. PFS censored at data cutoff. Median follow-up: 8.3 months.

Our comparative study provides several important insights for the clinical development of BRD4 inhibitors in hematologic malignancies. First, NVX-0228 demonstrated a favorable balance of efficacy and tolerability compared to OTX-015 and PLX-2853. The ORR of 38% in DLBCL and 40% in AML, combined with a relatively low rate of severe thrombocytopenia (8% Grade 3-4), positions NVX-0228 as a leading candidate for further development. Second, the identification of BRD4 phosphorylation at S492/S498 as a dominant resistance mechanism suggests potential combination strategies with CK2 inhibitors. Third, the enhanced activity in MYC-amplified tumors supports biomarker-guided patient selection.
